name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/17
units: metres below ground level; typed modern site-investigation log (Wimpey Geotech), descriptions transcribed verbatim

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff with occasional stiff pockets dark brown mottled orange brown occasionally thinly laminated sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium subangular gravel of coal and some fine and medium sand pockets
annotations: gravel of coal is reworked drift clast, not a seam

intervals.verbatim: Firm dark brown thinly laminated and occasionally orange brown mottled slightly sandy silty CLAY with some medium subrounded gravel of coal with dustings of silt on lamina surfaces and occasional small pockets of orange brown fine and medium sand
Firm dark grey thinly laminated silty CLAY with some polished lamina surfaces
annotations: hand-drawn water/seepage symbols in field-records ('If') column at approx 6.5-7.5m

intervals.verbatim: Stiff becoming very stiff dark brown slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine and medium subrounded gravel, occasionally thinly laminated with silt on lamina surfaces
annotations: water depth column reads DRY at 7.50 / 8.00m; hand-drawn seepage symbol near 8.5m

intervals.verbatim: Stiff locally very stiff brown mottled orange brown silty CLAY with some polished lamina surfaces

Medium dense dark grey brown clayey sandy SILT with some fine and medium subangular gravel of coal and occasional pockets of orange brown slightly clayey silt and occasional silty fine and medium sand pockets
annotations: spans sheet 1 to sheet 2 ('See previous sheet')

intervals.verbatim: Brown mottled grey yellow and orange brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND with occasional fine and medium subangular gravel of coal. Below 16.00m: dense
annotations: hand-drawn seepage symbols in field-records column near 17.0m

intervals.verbatim: Dense laminated brown very sandy SILT
Soft to firm dark brown thinly laminated locally slightly sandy silty CLAY with some silty partings
annotations: hand-drawn seepage symbols in field-records column near 18.8-19.1m; water depth column reads DRY at 19.00m

intervals.verbatim: Dense brown clayey silty fine to coarse SAND with some fine and medium subrounded to platy gravel of coal. Below 23.50m: with occasional pockets of brown silty clay
annotations: spans sheet 2 to sheet 3 ('See previous sheet')

intervals.verbatim: Firm grey brown mottled orange brown thinly laminated slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine subangular gravel of coal: very sandy in places
annotations: hand-drawn seepage symbols in field-records column near 26.5m

intervals.verbatim: Stiff with occasional firm to stiff pockets grey brown slightly sandy locally sandy silty CLAY locally thinly laminated with some fine and medium subangular gravel of coal
annotations: spans sheet 3 to sheet 4 ('See previous sheet')

intervals.verbatim: Dense grey brown mottled yellow brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND with some fine to coarse subrounded gravel
Yellow brown silty fine and medium SAND
Firm to stiff brown laminated silty CLAY
annotations: thin 0.10m parting between two sand-then-till units

intervals.verbatim: Stiff to very stiff grey brown sandy locally very sandy silty CLAY with some, becoming much fine and medium subrounded to subangular gravel and occasional cobbles
annotations: gravel-and-cobble diamict = glacial till (boulder clay); hole bottomed here
END OF BOREHOLE at 36.50m; water depth DRY at 35.00m

notes: Modern typed Wimpey Geotech cable-percussion log (Pilcon Wayfarer), Fig 18, commenced 15/6/92, 4 log sheets (Sheet 1-4 of 4) ending at 36.50m. Read 8 page images total: pages 1-4 = the strata log; pages 5-8 = site/location plans (Roughton & Partners, A167 Durham Western Bypass) with no strata, disregarded per rule 7. Entire section is Quaternary DRIFT (laminated glaciolacustrine clays/silts, glaciofluvial sands, a basal gravel-and-cobble till) — NO bedrock/Coal Measures rockhead proven; the 'gravel of coal' throughout is reworked drift clasts, not in-situ seams, so no coal, sandstone-post or seatearth beds and seam_marks is empty. OD-Level column cross-checks exactly against ground level 93.80 mOD at every one of the 17 boundaries (base OD = 93.80 - depth); no clerk arithmetic slips. Hole logged DRY throughout (DRY in water-depth column at 7.5/8.0, 19.0, 29.5/30.0, 35.0m); remarks note small amounts of water were ADDED to assist boring and a piezometer with lockable steel cover was installed. Hand-drawn seepage/water-strike style symbols appear in the narrow field-records ('If') column near 6.5-7.5m, 17.0m, 18.8-19.1m and 26.5m, recorded as annotations on the nearest intervals. Chiselling recorded 32.90-33.50m (1h), 34.00-34.50m (1h), 35.00-36.00m (2h) against the basal cobbly till. Location E5917.0 / N5918.0 (site grid); BGS header BNG 425913,540915.
